prompts.abstract: A rectangular plate with rounded corners and parallel edges.
prompts.description: A rectangular plate with rounded corners and parallel edges.
prompts.beginner: Design a thin rectangular plate with a length of about 0.75 units, a width of about 0.52 units, and a height of just over 0.003 units. This plate serves as the foundation of the model.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the default axes. Draw a rectangular shape on the first face, ensuring that the sides are parallel to the coordinate axes. After completing the rectangle, scale it down to 75% of its original size. Transform the scaled rectangle into a three-dimensional object by aligning it with the coordinate system. Extrude the rectangle along the normal direction to create a thin rectangular plate. The final dimensions of the plate are approximately 0.75 units in length, 0.52 units in width, and 0.0037 units in height. This process results in a solid body that forms the base of the model.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, draw the first line from the origin (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0), then draw the second line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.5192), followed by the third line from (0.75, 0.5192) to (0.0, 0.5192), and finally, draw the fourth line back to the origin (0.0, 0.0). After completing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three-dimensional space by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Next, extrude the transformed sketch 0.0037 units along the normal direction without extruding in the opposite direction. This operation will create a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular plate will be a length of 0.7500000000000002 units, a width of 0.5192307692307693 units, and a height of 0.0036923076923076927 units.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, draw the first line from the origin (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0), then draw the second line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.5192), followed by the third line from (0.75, 0.5192) to (0.0, 0.5192), and finally, draw the fourth line back to the origin (0.0, 0.0). After completing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three-dimensional space by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Next, extrude the transformed sketch 0.0037 units along the normal direction without extruding in the opposite direction. This operation will create a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular plate will be a length of 0.7500000000000002 units, a width of 0.5192307692307693 units, and a height of 0.0036923076923076927 units.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the default axes. Draw a rectangular shape on the first face, ensuring that the sides are parallel to the coordinate axes. After completing the rectangle, scale it down to 75% of its original size. Transform the scaled rectangle into a three-dimensional object by aligning it with the coordinate system. Extrude the rectangle along the normal direction to create a thin rectangular plate. The final dimensions of the plate are approximately 0.75 units in length, 0.52 units in width, and 0.0037 units in height. This process results in a solid body that forms the base of the model.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a thin rectangular plate with a length of about 0.75 units, a width of about 0.52 units, and a height of just over 0.003 units. This plate serves as the foundation of the model.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Plate

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Set the translation vector to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Draw the First 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Line 1:** Draw a line from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0).
    - **Line 2:** Draw a line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.5192).
    - **Line 3:** Draw a line from (0.75, 0.5192) to (0.0, 0.5192).
    - **Line 4:** Draw a line from (0.0, 0.5192) to (0.0, 0.0).

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the 2D sketch.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Translate the sketch using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch 0.0037 units towards the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction of the normal.
- This operation will create a new solid body.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.7500000000000002
- Width: 0.5192307692307693
- Height: 0.0036923076923076927
</part_1>
